rulings.Case Title: WOOD ROBBINS, LLP VS. HILDA LAI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Judgment In Conformance Therewith
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 1, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER WOOD ROBBINS, LLP's Hearing On Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Judgment In Conformance Therewith Plaintiff Wood Robbins, LLP's motion for order confirming arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 1, 2018, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT SWINERTON BUILDERS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendant Swinerton Builders' demurer to all three causes of action in the first amended cross complaint filed by C.I. Energia Solar S.A is overruled as to all three causes of action. The first and second causes of action adequately allege intentional and negligent misrepresentation claims based on Swinteron's alleged dissemination of the Project Manual knowing that Energia would rely on it and the Manual containing intentional and negligent misrepresentations about the testing of the window products. While the first cause of action adequately alleges an intentional misrepresentation claim, it also adequately alleges a concealment claim based on a misleading half-truth theory, i.e, Swinerton allegedly intentionally failed to disclose material facts to Energia that were needed by Energia to know the truth about the statements made in the Manual about the testing of the window products. The third cause of action adequately alleges tortious interference committed by Swinerton by its dissemination of the misrepresentations in the Manual which disrupted Energia's relationship with Bagatelos. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Energia is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DG VS. DOE 1 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 1, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANTs USA WEST PROVINCE, SOCIETY OF JESUS, and ST. IGNATIUS CHURCH's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants USA West Provence, Society of Jesus and St. Ignatius Church's demurrer to the fifth cause of action for fraud and the tenth cause of action for fraud and deceit in the second amended complaint is overruled as to the fifth cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the tenth cause of action. Paragraphs 57-59 of the SAC adequately support the fraud claim alleged in the fifth cause of action. The allegations regarding post abuse misrepresentations support a ratification theory of liability. The tenth cause of action is duplicative of the fifth cause of action and does not allege an independent viable claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the demurring defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
